join_order.notes: Build dense adsh filter and retain only EQ triples whose adsh passes SIC predicate.

join_order.build: pre(filtered: stmt='EQ' AND adsh in filtered sub)
join_order.probe: num rows from num_adsh_fk_hash postings for filtered adsh
join_order.notes: Use compact triple multiplicity map on (adsh,tag,version) and delay tag lookup until after first-stage aggregation.
Resolve tlabel once per surviving (tag,version) aggregate key instead of per qualifying num row.

data_structures.aggregation: two_phase_hash_group_by: local compact_hash_map((sic,tag,version,stmt)->{sum,count}) + local compact_hash_set((group_key,cik) inserted once per adsh-group), then final hash merge to (sic,tlabel,stmt)

optimization_notes: Root cause is residual wasted work in the hot path (Category C): main_scan still performs per-row tag lookups and repeated DISTINCT-cik insert attempts for the same adsh/group, while build_joins eagerly materializes tag mappings before knowing surviving aggregate keys. The revised plan keeps the selective sub+pre pruning and num_adsh index probing, but switches to a two-phase aggregation keyed by (sic,tag,version,stmt), records DISTINCT cik once per adsh-group, and resolves tlabel only for post-scan distinct (tag,version) keys with abstract=0 before final rollup to (sic,tlabel,stmt). This removes redundant per-row hash/set work and shifts tag join cost to work proportional to surviving groups.
